claim: Genomic instability in leukemia cells results from a decrease in reactive oxygen species from oncogene activation.

result.claim: Genomic instability in leukemia cells results from a decrease in reactive oxygen species from oncogene activation.

evidence.text: CD19-CAR-DNTs caused marked downregulation of CD19 expression in leukemia cells.
evidence.source: Development of multi-targeting CAR-double-negative T cells for allogeneic immunotherapy in B-cell leukemia

evidence.text: Pro-oxidant therapies can exploit ROS overload to selectively induce leukemic cell death.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Myelodysplastic Syndrome: Roles in Development, Diagnosis, Prognosis, and Treatment.

evidence.text: Upregulation of Aurora B kinase activity has been associated with genomic instability in human cells.
evidence.source: In vivo FRET imaging revealed a regulatory role of RanGTP in kinetochore-microtubule attachments via Aurora B kinase.

evidence.text: Monocyte-lineage cells produce reactive oxygen species.
evidence.source: Targeted deletion of CD244 on monocytes promotes differentiation into anti-tumorigenic macrophages and potentiates PD-L1 blockade in melanoma.

evidence.text: Decitabine (DAC) is known to induce cell-cycle arrest in leukemia cells.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Myelodysplastic Syndrome: Roles in Development, Diagnosis, Prognosis, and Treatment.

evidence.text: BCR-ABL1 fusion oncogene induces an increased production of reactive oxygen species.
evidence.source: DNA Repair—A Double-Edged Sword in the Genomic Stability of Cancer Cells—The Case of Chronic Myeloid Leukemia

evidence.text: Imbalanced metabolism and excess reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation can result in Parkinson's disease.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Neurodegenerative Diseases: A Review of Upstream and Downstream Antioxidant Therapeutic Options

evidence.text: Metaprofiles of oxidation-induced mutations from cancer patients on both strands of genes were analyzed.
evidence.source: Dynamic genome-wide mapping reveals how chromatin context shapes OGG1-mediated repair and related mutagenesis in human cells.

evidence.text: Oxidative stress promotes genomic instability.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Myelodysplastic Syndrome: Roles in Development, Diagnosis, Prognosis, and Treatment.

evidence.text: Increased formation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) has been noted in different types of myeloid leukemia.
evidence.source: NOX-driven ROS formation in cell transformation of FLT3-ITD positive AML

evidence.text: BCR-ABL1+ leukaemias tend to display constitutive active cytokine signalling via activation of STAT5 and repression of BCL6
evidence.source: Complex genotype-phenotype relationships shape the response to treatment of down syndrome childhood acute lymphoblastic leukaemia.

evidence.text: Elevated oxidative stress in patients with myelodysplastic syndromes has been linked to increased apoptosis of hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Myelodysplastic Syndrome: Roles in Development, Diagnosis, Prognosis, and Treatment.

evidence.text: The JAK2V617F mutation has been shown to induce the generation of reactive oxygen species (ROS).
evidence.source: Interferon-alpha2 treatment of patients with polycythemia vera and related neoplasms favorably impacts deregulation of oxidative stress genes and antioxidative defense mechanisms

evidence.text: Imbalanced metabolism and excess reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation can result in Alzheimer's disease.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Neurodegenerative Diseases: A Review of Upstream and Downstream Antioxidant Therapeutic Options

evidence.text: The transcriptomes of Rps15-mut pre-leukemic B splenocytes showed upregulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) genes with a NES of 1.5 and a p-value less than 0.05.
evidence.source: Impaired Response to Oxidative DNA Damage Underlies Genomic Instability in B-Cell Leukemia Driven By RPS15 Mutation

evidence.text: Increased formation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) is associated with leukemic cell proliferation and migration.
evidence.source: NOX-driven ROS formation in cell transformation of FLT3-ITD positive AML

evidence.text: HOX genes have a potential involvement in both cytopenias and leukemias.
evidence.source: MicroRNA target homeobox messenger RNA in HIV induced hematopoietic inhibition.

evidence.text: B-ALL–specific AS was found to affect 15 out of the 20 top leukemia driver genes from the COSMIC database.
evidence.source: An Alternatively Spliced Gain-of-Function NT5C2 Isoform Contributes to Chemoresistance in Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia.

evidence.text: Elevated oxidative stress in patients with myelodysplastic syndromes has been linked to increased apoptosis of hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Myelodysplastic Syndrome: Roles in Development, Diagnosis, Prognosis, and Treatment.

evidence.text: Decitabine (DAC) is known to induce cell-cycle arrest in leukemia cells.
evidence.source: Oxidative Stress and Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Myelodysplastic Syndrome: Roles in Development, Diagnosis, Prognosis, and Treatment.
